assessor_raw: The prediction model appears well-constructed: AUROC 0.81, meaningful top-decile lift, appropriate regularization for class imbalance. The problem is entirely in the inference the proposal draws from this performance. Predicting which subscribers will churn is a targeting problem. Whether discounting targeted subscribers reduces their churn is a causal estimation problem. The proposal treats the first problem as solved and assumes the second follows. It does not. The historical discount data would only provide causal evidence if discounts had been randomly assigned to model-equivalent customers — which they were not. The proposal recommends launching 'automatically' sending discounts to the top decile. Without a control arm, there is no way to measure whether the program works. The proposal should be restructured as a pilot proposal with a randomized holdout group, causal analysis plan, and a break-even analysis comparing discount cost to expected retained revenue.
